req.title: Manager creates employee
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a employee through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: Test suite validates manager's ability to create employees via UI form with mandatory field validation, audit logging, and proper HTTP responses. Coverage includes positive path (successful creation with 201 response and audit record), negative paths (validation errors, authentication/authorization failures), and boundary cases (field length limits, special characters). Equivalence partitioning segregates valid vs. invalid input classes. Decision table covers role-based access control. Boundary value analysis stress-tests field constraints.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_employee_with_valid_mandatory_fields
preconditions: manager authenticated with valid session token
employee table is empty or audit logging is active
actions: capture response status, response body (employee_id), and audit log entry
expected: audit record exists with action='CREATE', actor='manager', entity_id=<employee_id>, timestamp within 5 seconds
trace: R-WEB-001: expected outcomes—employee created, audit written, 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
def test_manager_creates_employee_with_valid_mandatory_fields(http_client, manager_session, audit_db):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_session['token']}"}
    payload = {"first_name": "John", "last_name": "Doe", "email": "john.doe@example.com", "department_id": 1}
    resp = http_client.post("http://target/employees", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    emp_id = resp.json()["id"]
    assert emp_id is not None and isinstance(emp_id, int)
    assert resp.json()["first_name"] == "John"
    audit = audit_db.find_one({"action": "CREATE", "entity_id": emp_id})
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit["actor"] == "manager"

tests.name: test_manager_create_employee_missing_mandatory_field_email
preconditions: manager authenticated with valid session token
email field is mandatory per schema
actions: capture response status and error message
expected: response body contains error message naming 'email' as missing or invalid
no employee record created in database
trace: R-WEB-001: error pathways—400 on validation error
R-WEB-001: mandatory fields must be validated

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_employee
expected: response body contains 'unauthorized' or 'authentication required' message
tests.executable:
def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_employee(http_client, employee_db):
    payload = {"first_name": "Bob", "last_name": "Wilson", "email": "bob@example.com", "department_id": 1}
    resp = http_client.post("http://target/employees", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    assert "unauthorized" in resp.json()["error"].lower() or "authentication" in resp.json()["error"].lower()
    count = employee_db.count_documents({"first_name": "Bob"})
    assert count == 0

tests.name: test_non_manager_role_forbidden_to_create_employee
actions: send request with authenticated token but non-manager role
expected: response body contains 'permission denied' or 'insufficient privileges'
trace: R-WEB-001: error pathways—403 if role forbidden

tests.name: test_manager_create_employee_email_field_boundary_max_length
preconditions: manager authenticated with valid session token
email field has maximum length constraint (e.g., 254 characters per RFC 5321)
actions: construct email with exactly 254 characters (valid format)
POST /employees with payload containing max-length email
expected: employee created with full email preserved
trace: R-WEB-001: boundary testing on email field length

tests.name: test_manager_create_employee_email_field_exceeds_max_length
preconditions: manager authenticated with valid session token
email field has maximum length constraint (e.g., 254 characters)
expected: response error message references email length or validation
trace: R-WEB-001: boundary testing—email exceeds max constraint
R-WEB-001: error pathways—400 on validation error

tests.name: test_manager_create_employee_first_name_empty_string
preconditions: manager authenticated with valid session token
first_name is mandatory and must be non-empty
expected: response error message indicates first_name is invalid or empty

tests.name: test_manager_create_employee_invalid_email_format
preconditions: manager authenticated with valid session token
email format validation is enforced (RFC 5322 or similar)
expected: response error message references email format or validity
trace: R-WEB-001: error pathways—400 on validation error
